english: transport, carry, to cross (a river) by boat or ferry, ču-bsgrál (chu bsgral) Lex.: तीर्ण passed over; ču-boi ṗá-rol-tu bsgrál-bar mdzód-čig (chu boi pha rol tu bsgral bar mdzod cig) have the goodness to take me over to the other bank Sambh.; ḳór-ba bsgrál-bai gru-rziṅs yin (khor ba bsgral bai gru rzings yin) Glr. it is a boat that carries over the river of transmigration. — 3. to remove, do away with, drive away, ˳dré-rnams ṗyii rgyá-mtso čėn-po-la bsgrál ('dre rnams phyii rgya mtsho chen po la bsgral) Glr. the demons were driven to the uttermost parts of the sea; bdud sgról-ba (bdud sgrol ba) to expel the devil; sdíg-ċan rgyál-po sgról-bar ˳gyur (sdig can rgyal po sgrol bar 'gyur) the guilty king will be removed out of the way! Glr.; dgra-bgegs bsgral-bai ṡa ḳrag rus-pa daṅ naṅ-rol glo snyiṅ bčás-pa mčod-par ˳bul (dgra bgegs bsgral bai sha khrag rus pa dang nang rol glo snying bcas pa mchod par 'bul) the flesh, blood, bones, heart, lungs and entrails of slaughtered enemies of the faith are offered by us as a sacrifice. This saying, the tendency of which is often justified by the sophism, that it is an act of mercy to kill an enemy of the faith and thus prevent him from accumulating more sin, shows that even 'mild Buddhism' is not incapable of bloody fanaticism, and instances like that of king Laṅ-dar-ma (lang dar ma) of old, and of the recent martyrdom of Roman Catholic christians at Bonga confirm this fact from experience.

sometimes also sgrol-yúm (sgrol yum) Cs., W. *ról-ma* (*rol ma*), 1. n. of two goddesses, Ssk. तारा, known in the history of Tibet as the white and green Tara, incarnated in the two wives of Sroṅtsaṅgámpo (srong btsan sgam po), Schl. 66 and 84; Köpp. II., 65. — 2. a name of females, of frequent occurrence.

1. Cs. manner, method, way, bṡád-sgros (bshad sgros) way of explaining, instructing, informing: sgrogs bṡád-sgros (sgrogs bshad sgros) Sch.: 'the method of instruction which is to be proclaimed' (?); gtám-sgros (gtam sgros) Cs.: 'way or manner of speaking' (?). — 2. Cs. edge, brim, lip; Sch. also mark from a wound, scar; żál-gyi mču-sgrós (zhal gyi mchu sgros) seems to signify only 'lip'.
= bźád-pa (bzhad pa) to smile, to smile on Stg.
1. v. rgál-ba (rgal ba), 2. v. rgól-ba (rgol ba).
Sch. 'das Gegenseitige', mutual relation, contrast, contrary?

a hundred, one hundred; brgya-ṗrág (brgya phrag) the hundred, a century; brgya-ṗrág bču (brgya phrag bcu) 1000; brgyá-pa (brgya pa) the hundredth; brgyá-po (brgya po) consisting of one hundred (cf. under dgu (dgu)); brgya daṅ bču-bźi (brgya dang bcu bzhi) 114; brgya-nyí-ṡu (brgya nyi shu) 120; bźi-brgya (bzhi brgya) (daṅ (dang)) go-brgyád (go brgyad) 498; brgyá-rtsa (brgya rtsa) v. rtsa (rtsa); brgyá-la (brgya la) (Cs.: brgya-ma-lan-˳čig (brgya ma lan gcig), or brgyá-lam-na? (brgya lam na?)) once among a hundred (cases or times) i.e. very rarely, e.g. (dus (dus)) brgyá-la brnyed kyaṅ (brgya la brnyed kyang) though it be found for once at last Mil. frq., cf. Schf. Erläut. zu Dzangl. p. 45; also = finally, in short, the Latin denique, Mil. nt.; brgyá-ċan (brgya can) erron. for rgyá-ċan (rgya can). — brgya-mčód (brgya mchod) a hecatomb of 100 lamps, offered on certain festival occasions Sik. — brgyá-˳daṅs (brgya 'dangs) about or nearly a hundred Sch. — brgyá-dpon (brgya dpon) a captain of a hundred men, the Latin centurio. — brgya-byín (brgya byin) (शतक्रतु) '(honoured by) a hundred sacrifices', epithet of Indra, cf. ἑκατόμβαιος) 1. Indra. 2. n. of a medicine Wdṅ.
1. in smrá-bai brgya (smra bai brgya) Sch.: noisy conversation; Lexx. simply आलाप speech, conversation (with the remark that the word is obs. and prov.). — 2. often erron for rgya (rgya).
1. v. rgyoṅ-ba (rgyong ba) 2. to call to a person from a distance, C.

eight; brgyád-pa (brgyad pa) the eighth, brgyad-po (brgyad po) consisting of eight, brgyád-ču (brgyad cu) eighty, brgyad-ču-rtsa-˳čig (brgyad cu rtsa gcig) (W. *gyad-ču-gya-čig* (*gyad cu gya cig*)), gya-˳čig (gya gcig) 81; brgyad-brgyá (brgyad brgya) 800; brgyad-stóṅ (brgyad stong) 8000; brgyad-ḳri (brgyad khri) 80 000.
a reproach, rebuke, brgyad-kág byéd-pa (brgyad kag byed pa) to rebuke, to chide Dzl.
vb. to adorn, decorate; to provide with (c. instrum.), cf. rgyan (rgyan) sbst.; nya mgo sá-yis brgyán-pa (nya mgo sa yis brgyan pa) the letter nya (ཉ) being provided with an s above it, = sny.., Zam.
1. to sink down senseless, to faint; *brgyál-te dúd-če* (*brgyal te dud ce*)
